message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker with concurrent agent updates

 {"id":"bd-gy4.8","title":"Unit tests: observability output format and field completeness","description":"Unit tests verifying all log/trace/evidence outputs contain mandatory fields (trace_id, decision_id), respect cardinality budgets, and produce valid JSONL.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:32.837850985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:49.574642233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenmermaid","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gy4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-gy4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gy4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-gy4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gy4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-gy4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-gy4.9","title":"E2E test: full pipeline with evidence ledger verification","description":"E2E test running full parse->layout->render pipeline and verifying that evidence ledger captures all decisions, trace continuity is maintained, and replay produces identical output.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:32.935224059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:41.661651006Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","frankenmermaid","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gy4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-gy4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gy4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-gy4.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-kgi4","title":"Adoption wedge: VS Code extension with live preview","description":"VS Code extension providing live Mermaid preview. Uses WASM build. Faster preview refresh than mermaid.js extension due to incremental computation.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:45.384602910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:45.384602910Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","frankenmermaid"]}
-{"id":"bd-ml2r","title":"[FNX] EPIC: Graph Intelligence Integration via franken_networkx","description":"Background\nfrankenmermaid already has deterministic parse->layout->render pipelines. franken_networkx (fnx) provides a high-performance algorithm engine plus CGSE witness artifacts. Integrating the two can materially improve structural diagnostics, layout decision quality, and observability if we stage the rollout carefully.\n\nCritical Constraint\nCurrent Rust fnx algorithms are primarily Graph (undirected) centric. Immediate wins should target undirected structural analysis and diagnostics. Directed-layout replacement must be treated as a later phase gated by explicit DiGraph coverage in fnx.\n\nEpic Goal\nAdd an accretive fnx-powered graph-intelligence layer to frankenmermaid that:\n1) improves diagnostics and layout decisions,\n2) preserves deterministic behavior,\n3) exposes explainable evidence/witness artifacts,\n4) avoids risky big-bang replacement of fm-layout internals.\n\nProgram Outcomes\n- Better parser/CLI diagnostics (components, articulation points, bridges, cycle basis).\n- Better layout tie-breaks and cycle-reversal scoring on dense/pathological graphs.\n- Witness-aware telemetry for algorithmic decisions and complexity costs.\n- Feature-gated rollout with deterministic parity checks and explicit success criteria.\n\nDefinition of Done\n- All child issues complete with acceptance criteria and test evidence.\n- Determinism checks pass repeatedly for representative corpus.\n- Quality gates pass (, , ) using rch offload.\n- CLI/WASM surfaces expose actionable fnx-derived diagnostics and decision traces.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:51:05.701089257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:51:05.701089257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","diagnostics","fnx","layout","parser"]}
-{"id":"bd-ml2r.1","title":"[FNX] Integration architecture contract and phased risk matrix","description":"Goal
Create the authoritative technical contract for integrating franken_networkx (fnx) into frankenmermaid without destabilizing current deterministic behavior.

Background
The project already has deterministic parsing/layout/rendering in Rust. fnx provides strong graph algorithms and witness artifacts but current Rust APIs are largely undirected. We need a staged plan that captures immediate wins and blocks unsafe assumptions.

Scope
- Define hard boundaries between Phase 1 (undirected structural intelligence) and Phase 2 (directed algorithm adoption).
- Define success metrics for quality, latency, determinism, and diagnostics usefulness.
- Define explicit rollback conditions and kill-switch behavior via feature flags.
- Produce implementation map across crates: fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-cli, fm-wasm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Written architecture contract embedded in repo docs and referenced by all child beads.
- Determinism contract and fallback behavior explicitly specified.
- Risk register includes algorithm mismatch, runtime overhead, and maintenance coupling.
- Clear go/no-go gates for each integration phase.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fnx","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ml2r","title":"[FNX] EPIC: Graph Intelligence Integration via franken_networkx","description":"Background\nfrankenmermaid already has deterministic parse->layout->render pipelines. franken_networkx (fnx) provides a high-performance algorithm engine plus CGSE witness artifacts. Integrating the two can materially improve structural diagnostics, layout decision quality, and observability if we stage the rollout carefully.\n\nCritical Constraint\nCurrent Rust fnx algorithms are primarily Graph (undirected) centric. Immediate wins should target undirected structural analysis and diagnostics. Directed-layout replacement must be treated as a later phase gated by explicit DiGraph coverage in fnx.\n\nEpic Goal\nAdd an accretive fnx-powered graph-intelligence layer to frankenmermaid that:\n1) improves diagnostics and layout decisions,\n2) preserves deterministic behavior,\n3) exposes explainable evidence/witness artifacts,\n4) avoids risky big-bang replacement of fm-layout internals.\n\nProgram Outcomes\n- Better parser/CLI diagnostics (components, articulation points, bridges, cycle basis).\n- Better layout tie-breaks and cycle-reversal scoring on dense/pathological graphs.\n- Witness-aware telemetry for algorithmic decisions and complexity costs.\n- Feature-gated rollout with deterministic parity checks and explicit success criteria.\n\nDefinition of Done\n- All child issues complete with acceptance criteria and test evidence.\n- Determinism checks pass repeatedly for representative corpus.\n- Quality gates pass (, , ) using rch offload.\n- CLI/WASM surfaces expose actionable fnx-derived diagnostics and decision traces.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:51:05.701089257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:55.304786885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","diagnostics","fnx","layout","parser"],"comments":[{"id":126,"issue_id":"bd-ml2r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation rationale snapshot (2026-02-27):\n1) Immediate high-ROI integration should focus on undirected structural diagnostics and advisory scoring, because current Rust fnx APIs are strongest in Graph-centric algorithms.\n2) Deep replacement of directed layout internals is explicitly deferred behind bd-ml2r.7.* capability gates to avoid correctness regressions.\n3) Determinism and explainability are non-negotiable; every fnx-assisted decision must have stable tie-break behavior and witness metadata available to CLI/WASM consumers.\n4) Rollout must remain feature-flagged until benchmark + determinism gates in bd-ml2r.8 are green.","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:55Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ml2r.1","title":"[FNX] Integration architecture contract and phased risk matrix","description":"Goal
Create the authoritative technical contract for integrating franken_networkx (fnx) into frankenmermaid without destabilizing current deterministic behavior.

Background
The project already has deterministic parsing/layout/rendering in Rust. fnx provides strong graph algorithms and witness artifacts but current Rust APIs are largely undirected. We need a staged plan that captures immediate wins and blocks unsafe assumptions.

Scope
- Define hard boundaries between Phase 1 (undirected structural intelligence) and Phase 2 (directed algorithm adoption).
- Define success metrics for quality, latency, determinism, and diagnostics usefulness.
- Define explicit rollback conditions and kill-switch behavior via feature flags.
- Produce implementation map across crates: fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-cli, fm-wasm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Written architecture contract embedded in repo docs and referenced by all child beads.
- Determinism contract and fallback behavior explicitly specified.
- Risk register includes algorithm mismatch, runtime overhead, and maintenance coupling.
- Clear go/no-go gates for each integration phase.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:55.357809748Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fnx","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: this task is the control plane for all downstream work. Do not begin adapter or diagnostics implementation without finalizing decision-contract semantics (advisory vs authoritative fnx outputs, fallback policy, and failure-mode behavior).","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1.1","title":"[FNX] Decide dependency model and feature-flag topology","description":"Goal
Decide exactly how frankenmermaid depends on fnx (workspace path, git dependency, or published crates) with reproducible builds and minimal coupling risk.

Scope
- Evaluate dependency strategies for local development, CI, and release packaging.
- Define feature flags (`fnx-integration`, `fnx-experimental-directed`) and default states.
- Specify how to build when fnx is unavailable.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cargo dependency strategy chosen and documented with rationale.
- Feature flags implemented in Cargo manifests with deterministic defaults.
- CI matrix includes fnx-on and fnx-off paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.934220363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.934220363Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","ci","fnx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.934220363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1.2","title":"[FNX] Deterministic decision contract and fallback semantics","description":"Goal
Codify deterministic decision rules for when fnx outputs are advisory vs authoritative so behavior remains stable and explainable.

Scope
- Define precedence between existing fm-layout heuristics and fnx-derived scores.
- Define deterministic tie-break policy and stable sorting keys.
- Define fallback semantics when fnx analysis errors, times out, or is unavailable.

Acceptance Criteria
- Decision table checked into docs with executable tests.
- Tie-break and fallback paths are deterministic and traceable.
- CLI diagnostics state when fnx advice was used, skipped, or degraded.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.993681995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.993681995Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","diagnostics","fnx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.993681995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.2","title":"[FNX] Implement Mermaid IR -> fnx graph adapter layer","description":"Goal
Build a robust adapter between MermaidDiagramIr and fnx graph structures.

Background
Integration quality depends on lossless, deterministic mapping of nodes/edges/clusters and stable IDs. Without this, all downstream algorithmic improvements are suspect.

Scope
- Implement adapter module that converts IR to fnx-ready graph representation.
- Preserve stable node/edge identity, directionality markers, and essential attributes.
- Provide explicit undirected projection utilities for algorithms that require Graph.
- Add round-trip integrity tests for mapping invariants.

Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter is deterministic across repeated runs.
- Identity and attribute mapping tests cover edge cases (self-loops, parallel edges, cluster-contained nodes).
- Conversion overhead is benchmarked and acceptable for target corpus.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.054535794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.381580807Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adapter","fm-core","fnx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.054535794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.381531405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
